revision.text:
[[ja:海犬養氏]] {{Infobox Japanese clan |氏名 = {{ill|Amanoinukai clan|ja|海犬養氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai clan|1=Ama-no-Inukai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55529579}} |image = |氏姓 = {{ill|Amanoinukai no Sukune|ja|海犬養宿禰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Sukune|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |始祖 = {{ill|Watatsumi|en|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|lt=Watatsumi|1=Watatsumi}}（{{ill|Watatsumi no Mikoto|ja|綿津見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}） |出自 = |氏祖 = |種別 = {{ill|Shinbetsu|ja|神別|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbetsu|qq=}}（{{ill|Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|ja|天津神・国津神|1=Kami#Amatsukami_and_Kunitsukami|2=ja|3=天津神・国津神|4=zh|5=天津神與國津神|6=ko|7=아마쓰카미·구니쓰카미|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}） |本貫 = {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}}{{ill|Naka District, Ibaraki|en|Naka District, Ibaraki|qid=Q1062968|lt=Naka District, Chikuzen Province|1=Naka District, Ibaraki}}{{ill|Ama District|ja|海部郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |人物 = {{ill|Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|ja|海犬養勝麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|1=Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51915273}}
{{ill|Amanoinukai no Okamaro|ja|海犬養岡麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Okamaro|1=Amanoinukai no Okamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51915279}}
{{ill|Amanoinukai no Ioyori|ja|海犬養五百依|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Ioyori|1=Amanoinukai no Ioyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q84465192}} |後裔 = }} '''Amanoinukai clan''' (あまのいぬかいうじ/あまいぬかいうじ) was a [[:en:氏|clan]] that bore the name "Amanoinukai." {{ill|Inukaibe|ja|犬養部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukaibe|qq=}} were a [[品部]] responsible for guarding the imperial gates and [[屯倉]], the direct holdings of the {{ill|Yamato court|ja|大和朝廷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato court|ja_comment=|2=ja|3=ヤマト王権|1=Yamato Kingship|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2658252}}, using trained dogs. The Amanoinukai were one of the six {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} families that oversaw them, along with {{ill|Agatanoinukai|ja|県犬養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Agatanoinukai|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 県犬養氏|1=Agata no Inukai clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11582689}}, {{ill|Wakainukai clan|ja|若犬養氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wakainukai clan|1=Waka Inukai Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55533707}}, {{ill|Azuminoinukai clan|ja|阿曇犬養氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azuminoinukai clan|1=Adon Inukai Clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q65291952}}, {{ill|Karatainukai no Muraji|ja|辛犬養連|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karatainukai no Muraji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and {{ill|Atamitenoinukai|ja|阿多御手犬養|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Atamitenoinukai|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Overview == The Amanoinukai clan (also called Amanokainukai) belonged to the {{ill|Ama People|ja|海神族|Ama Tribe=Ama People|lt=Kaijin clans|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama Tribe|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 海人族}}, a group claiming descent from the sea god {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|綿津見命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. They were considered kin to the {{ill|Azumi clan|ja|安曇氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇氏|1=Azumi people|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3377806}}.『{{ill|Shinsen Shōjiroku|en|Shinsen Shōjiroku|qid=Q1045362|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}』右京神別下 They were a {{ill|Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomonomiyatsuko|lt=Tomo no miyatsuko|ja|伴造|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tomo no miyatsuko|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} clan established by taking a portion of the {{ill|Ama no Be|ja|海人部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama no Be|1=Amabe Caste|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55528867}}, likely chosen as their overseers due to their connection with the Azumi clan.佐伯[1994: 32] Their original {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|qid=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} was {{ill|Muraji|en|Muraji|qid=Q2735611|lt=Muraji|1=Muraji}}, but following the {{ill|Yakusa no Kabane|ja|八色の姓|1=Eight Kabane system|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yakusa no Kabane|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kabane system|qq=}} reform of {{ill|684|en|684|qid=Q31128|lt=684|1=684}} ({{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} 13), they were granted the title of {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}}.『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』Emperor Tenmu13rd year 12月2日条 In {{ill|645|en|645|qid=Q31040|lt=645|1=645}} ({{ill|Empress Kōgyoku|en|Empress Kōgyoku|qid=Q235296|lt=Empress Saimei|1=Empress Kōgyoku}} 4), {{ill|Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|ja|海犬養勝麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|1=Amanoinukai no Katsumaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51915273}} participated in the assassination of {{ill|Soga no Iruka|en|Soga no Iruka|qid=Q736621|lt=Soga no Iruka|1=Soga no Iruka}} alongside {{ill|Saeki no Komaro|ja|佐伯子麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki no Komaro|1=Saeki no Komaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11382809}} and {{ill|Katsuragi no Waka Inukai no Amida|ja|葛城稚犬養網田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Katsuragi no Waka Inukai no Amida|1=Katsuragi no Wakainukai no Amita|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51914871}} during the {{ill|Isshi incident|en|Isshi incident|qid=Q1062771|lt=Isshi Incident|1=Isshi incident}}.『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』皇極天皇4th year 6月12日条 In {{ill|734|en|734|qid=Q23573|lt=734|1=734}} ({{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō era|1=Tenpyō}} 6), {{ill|Amanoinukai no Okamaro|ja|海犬養岡麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Okamaro|1=Amanoinukai no Okamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q51915279}} composed a poem praising the reign of {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}}, which was included in volume six, poem 996 of the {{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|萬葉集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Man'yōshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 万葉集|1=Man'yōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q834321}}.『{{ill|Man'yōshū|ja|萬葉集|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Man'yōshū|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 万葉集|1=Man'yōshū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q834321}}』二、p346 - p347　完訳　日本の古典3(小学館、1984th year ) During the {{ill|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu rebellion|en|Fujiwara no Hirotsugu rebellion|qid=Q5507515|lt=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu Rebellion|1=Fujiwara no Hirotsugu rebellion}} of {{ill|740|en|740|qid=Q23593|lt=740|1=740}} (Tenpyō 12), {{ill|Amanoinukai no Ioyori|ja|海犬養五百依|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Ioyori|1=Amanoinukai no Ioyori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q84465192}} served as a military scribe and led over twenty of Hirotsugu's captured followers.『{{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}』巻第十三、天平12nd year 11月15日条 Other members include {{ill|Amanoinukai no Hirotari|ja|海犬甘連広足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Hirotari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a scribe during the Tenpyō period (729–749), and {{ill|Amanoinukai no Hirotsukasa|ja|海犬甘連広主|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Hirotsukasa|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who served at the {{ill|Tōdai-ji|en|Tōdai-ji|qid=Q460367|lt=Tōdai-ji|1=Tōdai-ji}} scriptorium in {{ill|Jingokeiun 2|ja|神護景雲2年|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jingokeiun 2|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Unranked individuals include {{ill|Amanoinukai no Toyoshima|ja|海犬養豊島|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Toyoshima|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who appears in a decree from the {{ill|Naishidokoro|ja|内侍所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishidokoro|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 賢所|1=Kashiko-dokoro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11635085}} in {{ill|736|en|736|qid=Q23581|lt=736|1=736}} (Tenpyō 8), and {{ill|Amanoinukai no Maro|ja|海犬養麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amanoinukai no Maro|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a household head listed in the {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} tax record from {{ill|737|en|737|qid=Q411351|lt=737|1=737}} (Tenpyō 9). The Amanoinukai were based in {{ill|Chikuzen Province|en|Chikuzen Province|qid=Q1047664|lt=Chikuzen Province|1=Chikuzen Province}}, particularly in {{ill|Naka District, Ibaraki|en|Naka District, Ibaraki|qid=Q1062968|lt=Naka District, Chikuzen Province|1=Naka District, Ibaraki}}'s {{ill|Ama District|ja|海部郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ama District|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, near {{ill|Natsu no Kange|ja|那津官家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu no Kange|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, now part of {{ill|Minami-ku, Fukuoka|ja|福岡市南区三宅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minami-ku, Fukuoka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. This suggests they were responsible for guarding the {{ill|Natsu no Mikura|ja|那津の屯倉|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Natsu no Mikura|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The clan's name is preserved in the {{ill|Twelve Gates of the Imperial Palace|ja|宮城十二門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelve Gates of the Imperial Palace|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, specifically the {{ill|Ankamon|ja|安嘉門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ankamon|1=Ankamon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17988558}}. According to {{ill|Inoue Kaoru (historian)|ja|井上薫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoue Kaoru (historian)|1=Inoue Kaoru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11373214}}, these gates were named after clans that defended the {{ill|Asuka Itabukimiya|ja|飛鳥板蓋宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka Itabukimiya|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 板蓋宮|1=Itabuki Palace|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11532703}} and participated in the assassination of Soga no Iruka. {{ill|Saeki Arikiyo|ja|佐伯有清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Arikiyo|1=Saeki Ariyoshi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13364959}} expanded on this, suggesting that the twelve clans guarded specific gates due to their traditional military and provisioning duties.『{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}』（四）p402 - p403、岩波文庫、1995th year Additionally, {{ill|Ōbayashi Taryō|ja|大林太良|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōbayashi Taryō|1=Taryō Ōbayashi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3347920}} theorized that some Inukaibe also worked as {{ill|Ama (diving)|en|Ama (diving)|qid=Q1577970|lt=Ama diver|1=Ama (diving)}}, feeding their dogs with fish, which may have been the origin of the Amanoinukai.『{{ill|Japan’s Ancient Seafaring Traditions|ja|日本の古代8　海人の伝統|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japan’s Ancient Seafaring Traditions|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}』大林太良編、中公文庫、1996th year == References == {{reflist|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|Inukaibe|ja|犬養部|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inukaibe|qq=}} {{Japanese-history-stub|qq=}} {{people-stub|qq=}} [[ja:海犬養氏]] {{translated page|ja|海犬養氏|version=86160927|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q55529579}} [[Category:Japanese clans]] [[Category:Uji]] [[Category:Azumi people]]
